edges.description: Loss of osteoclast proton-pump function prevents normal bone resorption and drives skeletal sclerosis.
edges.source: Vacuolar Proton Pump Deficiency in Osteoclasts

edges.description: Progressive accumulation of unresorbed bone obliterates marrow space.
edges.target: Bone Marrow Failure from Medullary Obliteration

edges.description: Progressive skull sclerosis narrows cranial nerve foramina.
edges.target: Cranial Nerve Compression from Skull Thickening

nodes.description: Progressive accumulation of unremodeled bone obliterates the medullary cavity, causing loss of hematopoietic marrow space with resultant pancytopenia. Compensatory extramedullary hematopoiesis in liver and spleen causes hepatosplenomegaly.
nodes.id: Bone Marrow Failure from Medullary Obliteration

evidence.reference_title: Defects in TCIRG1 subunit of the vacuolar proton pump are responsible for a subset of human autosomal recessive osteopetrosis.
evidence.snippet: Infantile malignant autosomal recessive osteopetrosis (MIM 259700) is a severe bone disease with a fatal outcome, generally within the first decade of life

nodes.description: Progressive skull thickening narrows cranial nerve foramina, causing optic nerve compression (blindness), facial nerve palsy, and sensorineural hearing loss.
nodes.id: Cranial Nerve Compression from Skull Thickening

nodes.description: Failure of osteoclast-mediated bone resorption leads to progressive accumulation of unremodeled bone, obliterating the medullary cavity and narrowing cranial nerve foramina.

evidence.reference_title: Defects in TCIRG1 subunit of the vacuolar proton pump are responsible for a subset of human autosomal recessive osteopetrosis.
evidence.snippet: mutations in TCIRG1 are a frequent cause of autosomal recessive osteopetrosis in humans

nodes.description: TCIRG1 encodes the a3 subunit of the vacuolar H+-ATPase (V-ATPase), which is specifically expressed in osteoclasts and essential for proton secretion into the resorption lacuna. Loss-of-function mutations abolish osteoclast acidification capacity, preventing dissolution of hydroxyapatite mineral in bone matrix. Osteoclasts differentiate normally and are present in normal or elevated numbers but cannot resorb bone.
nodes.id: Vacuolar Proton Pump Deficiency in Osteoclasts

evidence.reference_title: Defects in TCIRG1 subunit of the vacuolar proton pump are responsible for a subset of human autosomal recessive osteopetrosis.
evidence.snippet: TCIRG1, encoding the osteoclast-specific 116-kD subunit of the vacuolar proton pump, is mutated in five of nine patients

evidence.reference_title: Defects in TCIRG1 subunit of the vacuolar proton pump are responsible for a subset of human autosomal recessive osteopetrosis.
evidence.snippet: Osteoclasts are present in normal or elevated numbers in individuals affected by autosomal recessive osteopetrosis, suggesting that the defect is not in osteoclast differentiation, but in a gene involved in the functional capacity of mature osteoclasts
